title: Human Biology: Body Systems
description: Understand how the human body works, from cells to organ systems and homeostasis.

quiz.prompt: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
quiz.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration, providing energy for cell functions. They are often called the 'powerhouse of the cell'.

quiz.prompt: Which of the following are functions of the human liver?

options.text: Regulating blood sugar via glycogen storage

quiz.explanation: The liver produces bile, filters toxins, and regulates blood sugar via glycogen. Red blood cells are produced in the bone marrow.

quiz.back: Homeostasis is the body's ability to maintain a stable internal environment despite external changes. Examples include regulating body temperature (36.5-37.5°C), blood pH (7.35-7.45), and blood glucose levels. It is achieved through feedback mechanisms.

quiz.prompt: How many chambers does the human heart have?
quiz.explanation: The human heart has 4 chambers: the left and right atria (upper) and the left and right ventricles (lower).

quiz.prompt: Which organs are part of the respiratory system?
quiz.explanation: The respiratory system includes the lungs, trachea, bronchi, and diaphragm. The liver is part of the digestive and metabolic systems.

quiz.front: What is the difference between DNA and RNA?
quiz.back: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) stores genetic information and is double-stranded with bases A, T, G, C. RNA (ribonucleic acid) carries instructions from DNA for protein synthesis and is single-stranded with bases A, U, G, C. DNA lives in the nucleus; RNA travels to ribosomes.

steps.title: Day 1: Cells and Energy
steps.description: Learn about cell structure and energy production.

steps.title: Day 2: Organ Systems
steps.description: Explore the heart, lungs, and liver.

steps.description: Understand how the body maintains balance.
